decks.notes: This deck focuses on using Allosaurus Rider to cheat out big creatures like Ghalta or Griselbrand early in the game using cards like Neoform and Eldritch Evolution. The deck also incorporates cards like Pact of Negation and Veil of Summer to protect its combo pieces. It includes a mix of ramp cards like Disciple of Freyalise and Nourishing Shoal to help accelerate its game plan. The deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with powerful creatures and disrupt their game plan with cards like Endurance and Consign to Memory.

The main focus of this deck is to quickly deploy artifacts onto the battlefield, using cards like Mox Opal and Mishra's Bauble to ramp up mana production. The deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with fast and aggressive plays, utilizing creatures like Kappa Cannoneer and Arcbound Ravager to deal damage quickly. The inclusion of cards like Tormod's Crypt and Shadowspear provide disruption and utility against opposing strategies. The deck's synergy with artifacts and the ability to generate value from sacrificing artifacts make it a formidable Aggro - Affinity archetype in Modern 1 vs 1 format.

This deck focuses on quickly ramping up mana and using the synergy between Amulet of Vigor and Primeval Titan to generate a massive board state and win the game in just a few turns by chaining together powerful land-based combos such as Scapeshift. Utilizing cards like Arboreal Grazer and Green Sun's Zenith to accelerate the deck's strategy, the goal is to overwhelm the opponent with big creatures and game-winning plays. With careful sequencing and planning, this deck aims to execute its combo efficiently and effectively to secure victory.

This Boros Aggro deck focuses on quickly overwhelming opponents with aggressive creatures and burn spells. It utilizes cards like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Seasoned Pyromancer to apply early pressure, while Phlage, Titan of Fire's Fury and Goblin Bombardment provide additional damage potential. Cards like Ajani, Nacatl Pariah and Voice of Victory help to buff and protect your creatures, while the inclusion of removal spells like Thraben Charm and Galvanic Discharge ensures you can deal with opposing threats efficiently. The deck aims to finish the game swiftly before opponents can stabilize.

This deck focuses on fast, aggressive gameplay using artifact creatures and various artifact support cards to overwhelm the opponent quickly. By combining efficient creatures like Arcbound Ravager and Krang, Master Mind with powerful artifacts like Mox Opal and Engineered Explosives, it creates a synergistic strategy that can quickly build a strong board presence. Emry, Lurker of the Loch provides card advantage and recursion, while cards like Metallic Rebuke and Tormod's Crypt disrupt opponents' strategies. The deck aims to apply pressure early and maintain tempo throughout the game to secure a victory.

The main theme of this deck revolves around utilizing a variety of creatures and spells to create powerful combos and synergies on the battlefield. By combining cards like No More Lies, Tishana's Tidebinder, and Quantum Riddler, the deck aims to control the board and outwit opponents. Additionally, the toolbox aspect allows for flexible and versatile gameplay, adapting to different situations and threats. With a mix of control, card advantage, and creature abilities, the deck aims to overwhelm opponents and secure victory through clever plays and strategic decision-making.

This deck focuses on using a combination of spells and abilities to control the opponent's board while setting up for a powerful combo finish with Goblin Charbelcher. By disrupting the opponent's plays with counterspells and removal, the deck aims to buy time to assemble the pieces needed to win the game in a burst of damage. The inclusion of Hydroelectric Specimen and Tameshi, Reality Architect help generate additional resources and provide alternative win conditions if necessary. This Landless Combo deck relies on careful resource management and sequencing of spells to achieve victory.

The deck focuses on utilizing UrzaTron lands to ramp into powerful Eldrazi and colorless spells, while controlling the board with removal and disruption cards. The primary win condition is to overwhelm the opponent with large threats like Ulamog, the Ceaseless Hunger and Ugin, Eye of the Storms, while using cards like Thought-Knot Seer and Sire of Seven Deaths to disrupt the opponent's game plan. Karn, the Great Creator is also included to provide additional utility and shut down artifact-based strategies. Overall, the deck aims to control the game and establish a dominant board presence to secure victory.

The deck focuses on quickly filling the graveyard with powerful creatures like Griselbrand and Emperor of Bones, then using cards like Goryo's Vengeance and Quantum Riddler to bring them back onto the battlefield for a game-winning combo. It also includes disruptive spells like Thoughtseize and Force of Negation to protect the combo. The deck aims to reanimate creatures at instant speed, catching opponents off guard and overwhelming them with a powerful board presence. With a mix of reanimation spells, disruptive elements, and powerful threats, the deck is built to surprise opponents and secure victory through a quick and devastating combo.

This Boros Aggro deck focuses on quickly overwhelming opponents with a mix of powerful creatures and direct damage spells. The goal is to establish early board presence with creatures like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Seasoned Pyromancer, while using cards like Phlage, Titan of Fire's Fury and Goblin Bombardment to finish off opponents. Ajani, Nacatl Pariah and Guide of Souls provide support and buffs for the creatures, while cards like Thraben Charm and Ranger-Captain of Eos offer utility and disruption. Overall, the deck aims to be fast, aggressive, and capable of closing out games quickly.

This deck focuses on playing aggressive creatures like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Scion of Draco to quickly deal damage to the opponent. It also includes cards like Lightning Bolt and Stubborn Denial for removal and protection, as well as Leyline of the Guildpact to boost the power of multicolored creatures. The deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with early pressure and efficient spells, while also utilizing cards like Fable of the Mirror-Breaker for card advantage. The land base is designed to support the diverse mana requirements of the deck.

The main idea of this deck is to control the game by countering opponent's spells, removing threats, and generating card advantage. It focuses on using a combination of counterspells, removal spells, and planeswalkers like Teferi to disrupt the opponent's game plan while maintaining card advantage through cards like Narset and Consult the Star Charts. The deck aims to control the board and out-value the opponent until it can secure the win condition, whether through a powerful planeswalker ultimate ability or overwhelming card advantage.

The main idea of this deck is to use cards like Ardent Plea and Living End to quickly cycle through your deck and bring out powerful creatures like Curator of Mysteries and Shardless Agent onto the battlefield. By discarding cards and manipulating your graveyard, you can trigger powerful effects and overwhelm your opponent with a sudden surge of creatures. The deck also includes counterspells like Force of Negation to protect your key combo pieces and disrupt your opponent's game plan. Overall, the deck focuses on setting up a lethal combination of creatures while disrupting your opponent's strategy.

The deck focuses on utilizing rituals and card draw spells to quickly generate mana and draw through the deck. The key strategy is to assemble the combo pieces (such as Ral, Monsoon Mage and Ruby Medallion) to generate a large storm count and finish off the opponent with a high-powered Grapeshot or strike it rich. The deck also includes some protection with cards like Wrenn's Resolve and Past in Flames to ensure success in executing the combo and overwhelming the opponent. The deck aims to win the game quickly and decisively through explosive turns.

This Dimir Control deck focuses on disrupting the opponent with a mix of early removal spells like Fatal Push and powerful counterspells like Counterspell and Force of Negation. The deck also utilizes card advantage engines like Snapcaster Mage and Consult the Star Charts to maintain control of the game. The win condition comes from finishing off the opponent with creatures like Murktide Regent and Orcish Bowmasters, backed up by disruptive spells like Sheoldred's Edict. The deck aims to control the game through counterspells, removal, and card advantage until it can secure a win.

The main focus of this UW Control deck is to maintain control over the game through countering opponent's spells and utilizing powerful planeswalkers like Jace, the Mind Sculptor and Teferi, Hero of Dominaria. The deck also includes cards like Narset, Parter of Veils and Spell Snare to further disrupt opponent's strategies. With a mix of removal spells like Supreme Verdict and Prismatic Ending, as well as card advantage engines like Geier Reach Sanitarium and Meticulous Archive, the deck aims to outvalue opponents while maintaining a solid defense.
